Atomic-write idiom: serialize to STATE_FILE.with_name(name+'.tmp') in same directory, then tmp.replace(STATE_FILE). Path.replace() = atomic rename on same filesystem (os.replace/MoveFileEx); handles dest-exists on Windows. Crash before replace → STATE_FILE untouched (old state preserved); leftover .tmp harmless (next write overwrites).

V1 5 repeated writes → valid JSON + dest-exists replace OK; V2 no .tmp leftover; V3 tmp same-dir as STATE_FILE (atomic-rename precondition); V4 non-serializable state → json.dumps raises → STATE_FILE preserved unchanged (crash-safety property confirmed)
